**Alexis Antonio Mendoza Barrina** (born November 8, 1961) is a retired Colombian [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football"), manager and the current assistant manager of [Honduras national football team](/wiki/Honduras_national_football_team "Honduras national football team")

He was the assistant manager of [Honduras national football team](/wiki/Honduras_national_football_team "Honduras national football team") during the [2010 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2010_FIFA_World_Cup "2010 FIFA World Cup") and of the [Ecuador national football team](/wiki/Ecuador_national_football_team "Ecuador national football team") during the [2014 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2014_FIFA_World_Cup "2014 FIFA World Cup"). Mendoza was capped 67 times and scored 2 international goals for [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia_national_football_team "Colombia national football team") between 1987 and 1997.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Club career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alexis_Mendoza&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Club career")]

Mendoza played most of his club career as a [defender](/wiki/Defender_(association_football) "Defender (association football)") for [Atlético Junior](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Junior "Atlético Junior"). He also played for [América de Cali](/wiki/Am%C3%A9rica_de_Cali "América de Cali") between 1990 and 1992, where he won two [Colombian league](/wiki/Colombian_Professional_Football "Colombian Professional Football") championships (1990 & 1992).

In 1993, he returned to Junior and helped them to win the 1993 and 1995 Colombian league championships.

Towards the end of his career Mendoza played for [Veracruz](/wiki/CD_Veracruz "CD Veracruz") in Mexico.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## International career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alexis_Mendoza&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: International career")]

Mendoza played one match at the [1994 World Cup](/wiki/Football_World_Cup_1994 "Football World Cup 1994"), and was an unused substitute for the [1990 World Cup](/wiki/1990_FIFA_World_Cup "1990 FIFA World Cup"). Mendoza also played in four editions of the [Copa América](/wiki/Copa_Am%C3%A9rica "Copa América") in [1987](/wiki/1987_Copa_Am%C3%A9rica "1987 Copa América"),[[3]](#cite_note-3) [1989](/wiki/1989_Copa_Am%C3%A9rica "1989 Copa América"),[[4]](#cite_note-4) [1993](/wiki/1993_Copa_Am%C3%A9rica "1993 Copa América")[[5]](#cite_note-5) and [1995](/wiki/1995_Copa_Am%C3%A9rica "1995 Copa América")[[6]](#cite_note-6)
